summary: Nostalgic audience tape of the Oakland show featuring a chilly “Cold Rain and Snow” opener, recorded on a TC‑153SD cassette with some tape‑flip quirks.
review: The recording is an old hand‑held cassette capture made by the reviewer in 1986, so it reflects the raw ambience of the Oakland venue rather than a polished soundboard mix. The show’s highlight mentioned is the cold‑weather “Cold Rain and Snow” opener, and the reviewer notes the tape‑deck handling (pausing between songs and an extra flip) which may affect continuity. Overall the tone is fond and positive despite limited technical detail.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one nostalgic review; sentiment is upbeat but no concrete audio quality info, so a moderate rating with low confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded on a TC‑153SD cassette deck; reviewer paused between songs and created an extra tape flip, suggesting possible gaps or uneven levels. No explicit comments on clarity or mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set ends with a tape pause and the second set starts after an extra flip, which may cause a noticeable break in continuity.
